free_text: Elastic amd Inelastic Scattering of 16O + 64Zn at Near-
Barrier Energies
The 16O beam extracted from a
Duoplasmatron ion source  was delivered by the 8UD 9
MV Tandem accelerator facility at the University of
Sao Paulo.
The Zn targets were made by evaporating Zn oxide by
electron  bombardment on a thin carbon backing (about
5 mu-g/cm2 thick). The  target thicknesses were within
the 30 to 40 mu-g/cm2 range. Three  targets of Zn and
a thin one of gold were mounted vertically at the
center of the scattering chamber.
an array of nine silicon surface
barrier detectors with angular  separation of 5 degr.
between them. apertures. Two silicon surface barrier
monitor detectors were mounted at +-15 degr. to
the beam direction.
The uncertainty of the experimental data
goes from 5% at forward  angles to 25% at the most
backward ones.

VS: EN-CM correction in 002.
The ratio to the Rutherford scattering was obtained
assuming that cross sections measured at forward
angles are pure Rutherford.
Data were digitized from fig.2 of the ref.
